content.full_text:
October 12, 1976


Ms. Melanie Manning
410 Ninth Street
Huntington Beach, California

Dear Ms. Manning:

Thank you for your letter of September 27 requesting information about the Political Reform Act. Enclosed are some informational fact sheets which should be of interest to you.

You may also be interested in attending an educational seminar we will be holding in Huntington Beach on October 18. I have enclosed a letter which outlines the details of when and where the meeting will be held.

Please do not hesitate to call me or our Los Angeles office (213/620-3196) if you require additional information.

Sincerely,


REGINA SICILIANO-KUTCHINS
Technical Assistance Program Coordinator


Enclosures
RSK:mak

bcc: Jeanne

The image appears to be almost entirely blank or white, with only two small curved marks visible near the top of the page that could be punctuation marks or artifacts. There is no discernible text content to transcribe.

410 - Ninth Street
Huntington Beach, California
September 27, 1976


Fair Political Practices Commission
1100 K Street
Sacramento, California 95814
Att: Mr. Daniel Lowenstein

Dear Mr. Lowenstein:

I am writing this letter on behalf of a group of Huntington
Beach residents who wish to form a non-profit organization
to study and act upon local political issues. Please
forward the necessary information relative to the Fair
Political Practices Act. Thank you for your prompt
reply to this request. At present we have no information
on this matter and do not wish to proceed without it.


Sincerely,

Melanie Manning

I see a mostly blank white page with two small circular elements near the top (appearing to be page holes or binding marks) and some very faint marks or specks in the upper right area. The rest of the page appears to be empty white space.

Since there is no legible text content to transcribe on this document, there is nothing to return per your instructions.
